text_blocks.answer: International Business Machines Corporation (IBM), nicknamed "Big Blue," is an American multinational technology and consulting company headquartered in Armonk, New York, with operations in over 175 countries. Founded in 1911 as the Computing-Tabulating-Recording Company (CTR) and renamed in 1924, IBM pioneered technologies like mainframe computers, the IBM Personal Computer, and FORTRAN. It holds the record for most annual U.S. patents for 29 consecutive years (1993–2021). IBM generates revenue from software, consulting, and hybrid cloud solutions, leveraging AI and platforms like Red Hat.

ai_overview.markdown:
## IBM

Technology company

International Business Machines Corporation (IBM), nicknamed "Big Blue," is an American multinational technology and consulting company headquartered in Armonk, New York, with operations in over 175 countries. Founded in 1911 as the Computing-Tabulating-Recording Company (CTR) and renamed in 1924, IBM pioneered technologies like mainframe computers, the IBM Personal Computer, and FORTRAN. It holds the record for most annual U.S. patents for 29 consecutive years (1993–2021). IBM generates revenue from software, consulting, and hybrid cloud solutions, leveraging AI and platforms like Red Hat.
